speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I came in today for my routine diabetes check-up.

speaker: doctor
text: Hello! Let's start with your vitals — your systolic blood pressure is 128 mmHg, which is within the normal range.

speaker: patient
text: That’s reassuring. I’ve been trying to stay consistent with my diet and walking.

speaker: doctor
text: Great to hear. Any new symptoms or concerns since your last visit?

speaker: patient
text: Actually, yes — I’ve had some dizziness, especially when standing up quickly, over the past few days.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m prescribing Metformin 1000mg twice daily to help manage your blood glucose — we’ll continue monitoring closely given the dizziness.

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate right now is 101 bpm — a bit elevated. Are you feeling anxious or unwell today?

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature is 36.1 °C — completely normal.

speaker: patient
text: I wasn’t aware my heart rate was that high — I do feel a little lightheaded, but no fever or chills.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s recheck your vitals in a few minutes after you’ve rested — dizziness plus tachycardia could suggest orthostatic changes or volume status issues.

speaker: doctor
text: Now your heart rate has dropped to 73 bpm — much more stable. That supports the idea that the earlier elevation was situational.

speaker: patient
text: Good — I did sit quietly for a couple of minutes. The dizziness hasn’t been constant, just intermittent.

speaker: doctor
text: We’ll also check your fasting glucose and HbA1c today. Has your home glucose monitoring shown any patterns?

speaker: patient
text: Mostly in the 130–160 mg/dL range before meals, but once last week it dipped to 68 — I had juice right away and felt better.

speaker: doctor
text: That hypoglycemic episode may be contributing to the dizziness — we’ll review your timing and dosing of current meds carefully.

speaker: patient
text: Makes sense. I’ve been taking my meds as prescribed, but maybe the timing needs adjusting?

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature is now 36.0 °C — still well within normal limits, consistent with our earlier reading.

speaker: patient
text: That matches the systolic number you gave me earlier — 128 mmHg — so at least my BP stayed steady between checks.

speaker: doctor
text: Exactly — stable BP and normalized heart rate after rest are encouraging. I’m going to switch you to Metformin 500mg twice daily for two weeks to assess if lower dosing reduces dizziness without compromising control.

speaker: patient
text: Okay, I’ll start that tomorrow and keep track of any dizziness or glucose readings. Thanks, Doctor!
